title: Springs, Clips, Paths, Fireflies & Popovers
description: CSS linear() spring easing, clip-path grid reveals, SVG textPath animation, canvas firefly trails, and the native Popover API.

drops.title: CSS linear() Spring Easing
drops.description: Compare spring, bounce, elastic, and classic easings side-by-side — all encoded in native CSS with the linear() function.
drops.concept: The CSS linear() timing function (2024) allows arbitrarily complex easing curves defined as a list of value stops. This replaces the need for JavaScript spring libraries for most motion use cases. The demo fires 5 balls simultaneously, each with a different custom easing, making the differences immediately visceral.

interaction.click: Fire All button fires all 5 balls simultaneously

drops.title: Clip-Path Grid Reveal
drops.description: Portfolio cards that reveal hidden content via clip-path: polygon() expanding from a single degenerate point to full coverage.
drops.concept: Animating clip-path: polygon() from a collapsed single-point state to a full rectangle creates a dramatic wipe-reveal that requires only CSS transitions. The technique uses cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1) for a spring-snap feel and can stagger per card. Portfolio and agency sites are using this pattern heavily in 2025/2026.

interaction.click: Click card to toggle reveal; Reveal All / Reset buttons

drops.title: SVG textPath Animation
drops.description: Text flows along three animated sine-wave SVG paths at different speeds and phases, creating layers of kinetic typography.
drops.concept: SVG's textPath element makes text follow any arbitrary path. By updating a sine-wave path via requestAnimationFrame and also animating the startOffset attribute, text appears to writhe and scroll along living curves. Three layers at different vertical positions, amplitudes, and speeds create rich parallax depth.
drops.source: MDN Web Docs — SVG textPath element

interaction.touch: Sliders to control amplitude, speed, frequency

drops.title: Firefly Cursor Trail
drops.description: Canvas particles bloom, drift, and fade as the cursor moves. Click for a burst explosion. Five palettes, tunable density and size.
drops.concept: A canvas layer tracks pointer/touch events and spawns glowing radial-gradient particles on each move event (throttled to every 2nd). Each particle has velocity, gravity, and alpha-decay, rendering as a bright core plus a large soft halo. Click triggers a burst of 30 particles in all directions. The cursor is replaced by a custom CSS dot.

interaction.mouse: Move to trail, click for burst
interaction.touch: Touch and drag for trail, tap for burst

drops.title: Native Popover API
drops.description: Tooltips, dropdowns, and backdropped panels using only the HTML popover attribute — with CSS @starting-style enter/exit animations.
drops.concept: The HTML popover attribute (baseline 2024) gives any element top-layer rendering, focus management, light-dismiss, and Esc-to-close for free. Combined with CSS @starting-style for 'from' state and transition: display allow-discrete for 'to hidden' state, you get smooth enter/exit transitions purely in CSS. The demo shows three popover variants: auto-dismiss tooltip, dropdown menu, and manual modal panel.

interaction.mouse: Click buttons to open each popover type
interaction.click: Buttons toggle popovers; outside click light-dismisses auto popovers
interaction.touch: Touch to open; touch outside to dismiss
